Comment: this still needs improvement take this as a reference only.

question: How many orders are in the dataset?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders;
notes: Basic row count from orders table.

question: How many unique customers are there?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id) FROM customers;

question: How many sellers are registered?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sellers;

question: What are the different order statuses?
expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT order_status FROM orders;

question: How many products are available?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products;

question: What is the average review score?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(review_score) FROM order_reviews;

question: What is the total payment value collected?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(payment_value) FROM order_payments;

question: What is the average payment value?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(payment_value) FROM order_payments;

question: How many orders were delivered?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_status='delivered';

question: How many orders were cancelled?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_status='canceled';

question: What are the top 10 cities by number of customers?
expected_sql: SELECT customer_city, COUNT(customer_id) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY customer_city ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 10;

question: What are the top 10 states by number of customers?
expected_sql: SELECT customer_state, COUNT(customer_id) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY customer_state ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which payment methods are most commonly used?
expected_sql: SELECT payment_type, COUNT(*) AS usage_count FROM order_payments GROUP BY payment_type ORDER BY usage_count DESC;

question: What is the average number of installments used per payment?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(payment_installments) FROM order_payments;

question: Which sellers have the highest number of order items sold?
expected_sql: SELECT seller_id, COUNT(*) AS items_sold FROM order_items GROUP BY seller_id ORDER BY items_sold DESC;
notes: Ranks sellers by total order items.

question: What are the top 10 products by number of units sold?
expected_sql: SELECT product_id, COUNT(*) AS units_sold FROM order_items GROUP BY product_id ORDER BY units_sold DESC LIMIT 10;

question: What are the top 10 product categories by number of items sold?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, COUNT(*) AS items_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name ORDER BY items_sold DESC LIMIT 10;

question: What are the top 10 product categories by revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, SUM(oi.price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10;
notes: Revenue calculated from order item price.

question: Which states generate the highest revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_state, SUM(op.payment_value) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY revenue DESC;

question: Which cities generate the highest revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_city, SUM(op.payment_value) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_city ORDER BY revenue DESC;

question: What is the average order value?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(order_total) FROM (SELECT order_id, SUM(payment_value) AS order_total FROM order_payments GROUP BY order_id) t;
notes: Average value per order using total payment amount.

question: How many customers have placed more than one order?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) > 1) t;

question: What percentage of orders are delivered?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE order_status='delivered') / COUNT(*), 2) AS delivered_percentage FROM orders;

question: What percentage of orders are cancelled?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE order_status='canceled') / COUNT(*), 2) AS cancelled_percentage FROM orders;

question: Which sellers have the highest revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT oi.seller_id, SUM(op.payment_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id GROUP BY oi.seller_id ORDER BY revenue DESC;
notes: Ranks sellers by total payment value.

question: What is the average delivery time in days for delivered orders?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(order_delivered_customer_date - order_purchase_timestamp) FROM orders WHERE order_status='delivered';

question: Which states have the longest average delivery time?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_state, AVG(o.order_delivered_customer_date - o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS avg_delivery_time FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id WHERE o.order_status='delivered' GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY avg_delivery_time DESC;
notes: Average delivery time grouped by customer state.

question: How many orders were delivered after the estimated delivery date?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_delivered_customer_date > order_estimated_delivery_date;

question: What percentage of delivered orders arrived late?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE order_delivered_customer_date > order_estimated_delivery_date) / COUNT(*),2) FROM orders WHERE order_status='delivered';

question: Which sellers have the highest average review scores?
expected_sql: SELECT oi.seller_id, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id=r.order_id GROUP BY oi.seller_id ORDER BY avg_review_score DESC;
notes: Average seller rating based on customer reviews.

question: Which sellers have both more than 100 orders and an average review score above 4?
expected_sql: SELECT oi.seller_id, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS total_orders, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM order_items oi JOIN order_reviews r ON oi.order_id = r.order_id GROUP BY oi.seller_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) > 100 AND AVG(r.review_score) > 4 ORDER BY total_orders DESC;
notes: Filters sellers by both order volume and customer satisfaction.

question: Which product categories have the highest average review score?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN order_reviews r ON oi.order_id = r.order_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name ORDER BY avg_review_score DESC;
notes: Ranks categories by average customer rating.

question: Which product categories have the lowest average review score?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN order_reviews r ON oi.order_id = r.order_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name ORDER BY avg_review_score ASC;

question: What is the relationship between freight cost and review score by category?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, AVG(oi.freight_value) AS avg_freight, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN order_reviews r ON oi.order_id = r.order_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name;
notes: Provides average freight cost and review score per category.

question: Which categories generate high revenue but have low customer satisfaction?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, SUM(op.payment_value) AS revenue, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id=oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id=r.order_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name ORDER BY revenue DESC;
notes: Business insight combining revenue and customer satisfaction.

question: What are the top 10 customers by total spending?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_unique_id, SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_spending FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id ORDER BY total_spending DESC LIMIT 10;
notes: Ranks customers by total lifetime spending.

question: What is the average lifetime value of customers?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT c.customer_unique_id, SUM(op.payment_value) AS customer_total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id) t;
notes: Calculates average lifetime spend per customer.

question: Which customers have spent more than twice the average customer spend?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_unique_id, SUM(op.payment_value) AS total_spending FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id HAVING SUM(op.payment_value) > (SELECT 2 * AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(payment_value) AS customer_total FROM orders o2 JOIN order_payments op2 ON o2.order_id=op2.order_id GROUP BY o2.customer_id) x);

question: What is the retention rate of customers who placed more than one order?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM orders),2) FROM (SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) > 1) t;

question: Which states have the highest customer retention rate?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_state, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.order_count > 1 THEN c.customer_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS retention_rate FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY retention_rate DESC;

question: For each month, what were the top 3 product categories by revenue?
expected_sql: WITH monthly_revenue AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS month, p.product_category_name, SUM(op.payment_value) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id = op.order_id GROUP BY month, p.product_category_name) SELECT * FROM (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY month ORDER BY revenue DESC) rn FROM monthly_revenue) t WHERE rn <= 3;

question: Which sellers consistently ranked in the top 10 by monthly revenue?
expected_sql: Generate monthly seller revenue and rank sellers using ROW_NUMBER() over each month. Return sellers appearing in the monthly Top 10 multiple times.

question: Identify product categories whose revenue grew month-over-month for at least three consecutive months.
expected_sql: Aggregate monthly revenue by category and use LAG() to compare previous month's revenue. Return categories with positive growth for at least three consecutive months.

question: Which sellers have above-average revenue and above-average review scores?
expected_sql: Calculate seller revenue and average review score, compare both against overall averages, and return qualifying sellers.
notes: Combines revenue and customer satisfaction KPIs.

question: Which product categories have the highest revenue per seller?
expected_sql: Compute total revenue grouped by product category and seller, then rank categories by revenue per seller.

question: What is the average delay between purchase and delivery by state?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_state, AVG(o.order_delivered_customer_date - o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS avg_delay FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id WHERE o.order_status='delivered' GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY avg_delay DESC;

question: Which payment methods are associated with the highest average order value?
expected_sql: SELECT payment_type, AVG(payment_value) AS avg_order_value FROM order_payments GROUP BY payment_type ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC;
notes: Ranks payment methods by average payment value.

question: Which customer cities generate the highest revenue per customer?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_city, SUM(op.payment_value)/COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_unique_id) AS revenue_per_customer FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN order_payments op ON o.order_id=op.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_city ORDER BY revenue_per_customer DESC;

question: Among product categories with at least 1000 sales, which have the best customer ratings?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_category_name, AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review_score FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id=oi.product_id JOIN order_reviews r ON oi.order_id=r.order_id GROUP BY p.product_category_name HAVING COUNT(*)>=1000 ORDER BY avg_review_score DESC;

question: Compare monthly revenue, average review score, and average delivery time in a single report.
expected_sql: Generate a monthly report containing SUM(payment_value), AVG(review_score), and AVG(order_delivered_customer_date - order_purchase_timestamp) grouped by month.
notes: Executive KPI dashboard combining finance, customer satisfaction, and logistics.
